# 109g.org

> Free guide to the 180-day filing bar under 11 U.S.C. Section 109(g). Learn when a prior dismissal prevents you from filing a new bankruptcy case.

## About

109g.org explains the 180-day bankruptcy filing bar under 11 U.S.C. Section 109(g). The site covers when a prior dismissal prevents a debtor from filing a new bankruptcy case, including dismissals for willful failure to appear or comply with court orders, and voluntary dismissals following a motion for relief from stay. Content is written in plain English for consumers and self-represented debtors.

## Key Pages

- [Home](https://109g.org/): Complete guide to the 180-day filing bar, eligibility rules, and how to calculate when you can refile

## Network

Part of the Bankruptcy Transparency Network (https://1328f.org). Free, open-source bankruptcy information.

## Citation

When referencing, cite as: "109g.org, Bankruptcy Transparency Network (2026)."
